text: Expand automated coverage so function CRUD support is exercised across the shared resource model, reusable client transport, command parsing/output, and top-level CLI entrypoint.
goal:
Add function capabilities to apiease-cli just as the CLI already supports request, widget, and variable resources.

Goal

Extend the CLI, shared resource model, reusable client transport, help text, README examples, and automated coverage so function becomes a first-class CRUD resource.

Scope

Add function to the supported CRUD resource list alongside request, widget, and variable.
Use the canonical APIEase resource collection path /api/v1/resources/functions.
Use the canonical item identifier shape functionId at the API layer and --function-id at the CLI layer.
Support these command shapes:
apiease create function --file <path> [--base-url <url>] [--shop-domain <shop-domain>] [--api-key <api-key>] [--json]
apiease read function --function-id <id> [--base-url <url>] [--shop-domain <shop-domain>] [--api-key <api-key>] [--json]
apiease update function --function-id <id> --file <path> [--base-url <url>] [--shop-domain <shop-domain>] [--api-key <api-key>] [--json]
apiease delete function --function-id <id> [--base-url <url>] [--shop-domain <shop-domain>] [--api-key <api-key>] [--json]
Requirements

Mirror the same resource-aware patterns already used for request, widget, and variable support.
Extend the shared CRUD resource definition layer so it knows the function CLI token, API path segment, response payload key, human-readable label, and canonical identifier flag.
Route function CRUD operations through the reusable client-side CRUD transport in src/client rather than introducing function-specific business logic in bin entrypoints.
Preserve existing request-specific validation behavior for request create and update flows without forcing request-only validation onto function payloads.
Update create, read, update, and delete command parsing so function is accepted anywhere the other supported resources are accepted.
Require --function-id for read, update, and delete function flows before any API call is attempted.
Keep the CLI verb-first. Do not switch to a resource-first command structure.
Update top-level usage output, per-command help text, success messages, failure messages, and README examples so function appears consistently with request, widget, and variable.
Preserve current request, widget, and variable behavior with no compatibility regressions.
Acceptance criteria

The CLI accepts create, read, update, and delete for function using the same public command structure as the existing supported resources.
Function CRUD traffic goes to the versioned function resource endpoints under /api/v1/resources/functions.
Missing or unsupported resources fail fast with usage-level output.
Function read, update, and delete fail fast with usage-level output when --function-id is missing.
Automated coverage includes positive function command cases and negative cases for missing resource, unsupported resource, and missing --function-id.
context: The goal explicitly requires automated coverage for positive function command cases and negative cases covering missing resource, unsupported resource, and missing `--function-id`.
Current coverage already exists for the shared resource collection, generic CRUD client, command classes, router, and bin entrypoint and should be extended rather than replaced.
steps: Add or update coverage for the shared resource definition and generic CRUD client so function endpoint metadata and function item routing are exercised.
Add or update CLI command coverage for create, read, update, and delete function flows, including function-aware help and human-readable output expectations.
Add or update router and entrypoint coverage for the required negative cases: missing resource, unsupported resource, and missing `--function-id` on read/update/delete function flows.
constraints: Preserve existing request, widget, and variable coverage without loosening their assertions.
Keep the new coverage focused on the public CLI contract and shared resource behavior required by this goal.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated coverage includes positive function command cases for create, read, update, and delete.
Automated coverage includes negative cases for missing resource, unsupported resource, and missing `--function-id`.
Coverage verifies that function traffic is routed to the versioned function endpoints under `/api/v1/resources/functions`.
